sections.paragraph:
pr. subst. what is over against, in sight, used as a prep.

(1) over against, opposite, Exo 26:35 40:24 Jos 15:7 18:17 1Ki 20:29.

(2) before. נֹכַח יְהֹוָה i.q. לִפְנֵי יְהֹוָה before Jehovah, i.e. acceptable to him, Jud. 18:6 . נֹכַח פְּנֵי יְהֹוָה before Jehovah, Lam 2:19 metaph. known to him, Jer 17:16 compare Pro 5:21. שׂוּם נֹכַח פָּנִים to put (any thing) before one’s own face, i.e. regard it with favour, to delight in it, Eze 14:7 (verse Eze 14:3for שׂוּם there is נָתַן).

With prefixes-

(1) אֶל נֹכַח pr. towards the face or front of, any thing; towards, Num 19:4.

(2) לְנֹכַח-(a) adv. towards what is opposite, i.e. straight before oneself (gerade vor fich, Luth. ftracks) Pro 4:25.-(b) before, Gen 30:38 hence-(c) for (comp. Germ. vor and für, the latter of which has properly a local signification), used after a verb of interceding, Gen 25:21.

(3) עַד נֹכַח unto, even to (the place which is) over against, Jud. 19:20 Eze 47:20.
